input.background: In 1954, the prominent statistician W. G. Cochran (1909-1980) published an important article on the analysis of cross-tabulated counts. On page 435 of the article, techniques were illustrated with an example from a study on the treatment of leprosy. Table 18.10 lists the cross-tabulated results. The row variable in this table classifies patients according to their initial degree of skin damage. The column variable classifies overall response to therapy.
input.question: Comment on these findings.

output.answer: These distributions show that patients with high skin damage were more likely to show improvement than those with low skin infiltration.

Skin_infiltration.description: The initial degree of skin damage in leprosy patients at the onset of treatment.

Response_to_treatment.description: The overall response to therapy in leprosy patients.

High_counts.description: Counts of patients with high skin infiltration for each response category.

Low_counts.description: Counts of patients with low skin infiltration for each response category.

Total_counts.description: Total counts of patients for each response category across all skin infiltration levels.

Overall_total.description: The total number of patients in the study.
